headline: Micron (MU) Stock Faces Michael Burry Short As Legal Risks Linger
summary: Michael Burry has disclosed a short position against Micron Technology (NasdaqGS:MU), targeting one of the market's closely watched AI hardware stocks. The move comes as Micron reports record earnings supported by AI-related memory demand and long-term customer agreements. Investor attention is also on an active class-action lawsuit over alleged price fixing and recent insider share sales, which have added to share price volatility. Micron Technology sits at the center of the AI hardware...

headline: Forget Micron? This New AI Memory Stock Could Be the Best Buy of the Decade
summary: Artificial intelligence has created no shortage of investment opportunities, but it has also exposed one bottleneck after another. First it was GPUs. Then networking. Then power generation. Today, memory has become one of the industry’s biggest constraints. Every advanced AI model requires enormous amounts of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and DRAM to keep increasingly powerful chips ... Forget Micron? This New AI Memory Stock Could Be the Best Buy of the Decade

headline: Why Michael Burry’s Massive Bet Against Micron Could Be His Biggest Mistake Yet
summary: The market has a habit of rewarding investors who recognize when an old pattern no longer applies. Cyclical industries eventually boom and bust, but structural shifts can stretch those cycles far longer than anyone expects. Artificial intelligence has done exactly that for semiconductors. Companies supplying the hardware behind AI training are no longer serving ordinary ... Why Michael Burry’s Massive Bet Against Micron Could Be His Biggest Mistake Yet

headline: Micron Risks Falling Out of the Trillion-Dollar Club. AMD Stock Has a Clear Path to Joining.
summary: Micron's $1 trillion valuation is being tested as investors question how long AI memory shortages will last. AMD's broader AI business could help it reach the $1 trillion club, though both companies remain positioned for long-term growth.
